title: famatina andina
intro: Famatina andina, also known as Chilean boxwood or Chilean box, is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation, thanks to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Famatina andina include its potential to improve cardiovascular health, reduce anxiety, and even exhibit antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, Famatina andina is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being applied topically in the form of creams and ointments.
